articles.description: Wisconsin center Jake Renfro suffered what school officials described as a lower-body injury in practice, putting his status for the start of the season in question.
articles.headline: Wisconsin center Renfro in question for opener with injury

images.name: Deion Sanders announces bladder cancer battle
images.caption: Colorado coach Deion Sanders and his medical crew reveal that he battled and beat bladder cancer.

articles.description: After a recent cancer scare, Colorado coach Deion Sanders says he's feeling "healthy and vibrant" ahead of his team's season opener against Georgia Tech on Aug. 29 in Boulder.
articles.headline: 'Healthy, vibrant' Sanders expects Colorado to 'win differently'

images.name: Arch Manning [1296x729]
images.caption: Arch Manning now has the fifth-shortest odds to be the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NFL draft after his grandfather's comments on Thursday.

images.name: Jacob Misiorowski [1296x729]
images.caption: Jacob Misiorowski's odds to win NL Rookie of the Year have fallen from -220 to +900.

images.name: Dak Prescott of Dallas Cowboys [608x342]
images.caption: The Cowboys won seven games last year, and sportsbooks aren't too keen on them doing much better this season.

images.name: Should Arch Manning stay at Texas beyond this season?
images.caption: The "Get Up" crew breaks down Arch Manning's path to the NFL and debates whether he should stay at Texas after this season.

articles.description: Arch Manning's odds to be the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NFL draft drastically shifted after Archie Manning said his grandson would stay at Texas.
articles.headline: Betting buzz: Manning no longer favorite to go No. 1 in 2026 NFL draft

articles.description: Boise State, Colorado State and Utah State filed an updated lawsuit arguing the Mountain West and commissioner Gloria Nevarez willfully disregarded the league's bylaws by "intentionally and fraudulently" depriving the schools of their membership rights.
articles.headline: Boise State, Colorado State, Utah State sue Mountain West

articles.description: It's time for a new season of "College GameDay" on ESPN! Check out the event hub page for weekly site details, recaps and other information.
articles.headline: Where is 'College GameDay'? 2025 schedule, locations, recaps

images.name: Garrett Nussmeier of the LSU Tigers warms up before a game against the Baylor Bears in the Kinder's Texas Bowl at NRG Stadium on December 31, 2024 in Houston, Texas. [600x400]
images.alt: Garrett Nussmeier of the LSU Tigers warms up before a game against the Baylor Bears in the Kinder's Texas Bowl at NRG Stadium on December 31, 2024 in Houston, Texas.

images.name: Kelly: QB Nussmeier's run game will be crucial to LSU's success
images.caption: The SEC Now crew delves into Garrett Nussmeier's potential on the run and the Tigers' new approach, specifically to their high-stakes opener against Clemson.

articles.description: LSU quarterback Garrett Nussmeier is dealing with patellar tendinitis in his knee after aggravating the issue in practice this week, according to coach Brian Kelly.
articles.headline: LSU QB Garrett Nussmeier dealing with patellar tendinitis

videos.description: Karan Higdon runs for 200 yards and three touchdowns, including the winner in overtime, as No. 17 Michigan defeats Indiana  27-20.
videos.headline: Michigan survives with OT win over Indiana

videos.description: On a 51-yard field goal attempt, Hoosiers kicker Griffin Oakes has his kick blocked by Maurice Hurst, and Lavert Hill returns it back 35 yards all the way down to the Indiana 27-yard line.
videos.headline: Michigan blocks Indiana FG attempt

videos.description: Karan Higdon takes a handoff and beats the defenders to the pylon to score a 12-yard touchdown, giving the Wolverines a 13-0 lead.
videos.headline: Higdon gets to the edge for Michigan TD

videos.description: Karan Higdon finds a big hole in the line and takes it untouched all the way to the house for a touchdown, giving Michigan a 20-10 lead.
videos.headline: Higdon breaks free for 59-yard Wolverines TD

videos.description: On third-and-goal, Peyton Ramsey finds Whop Philyor over the middle and Philyor dives toward the pylon for an eight-yard Indiana touchdown,  cutting Michigan's lead to 20-17.
videos.headline: Hoosiers score as Philyor dives into end zone

videos.description: Karan Higdon rushes for his third touchdown of the game, scoring from 25 yards out to give Michigan a 27-20 lead in overtime.
videos.headline: Wolverines strike first in overtime

videos.description: On fourth-and-goal in overtime, Indiana QB Peyton Ramsey throws a jump ball into the end zone and Michigan's Tyree Kinnel picks it off, clinching a 27-20 Wolverines win.
videos.headline: Michigan INT in end zone seals victory

videos.description: Michigan QB John O'Korn takes a deep shot on first down, but overthrows Donovan Peoples-Jones on a would-be touchdown.  The drive would end in a Wolverines field goal.
videos.headline: Michigan airs it out, but can't connect on deep ball

videos.description: On third-and-seven, Wolverines QB John O'Korn breaks a tackle in the pocket, scrambles right, and throws off-balance to Donovan Peoples-Jones who leaps up to make a 17-yard catch for a first down.
videos.headline: O'Korn evades pressure, completes pass for Michigan third down conversion

videos.description: Morgan Ellison finds a big hole in the line and turns on the jets to rush for a gain of 31 yards.
videos.headline: Ellison bursts out of the backfield for big Indiana gain

videos.description: Morgan Ellison caps Indiana's drive with an 8-yard touchdown run up the middle, cutting Michigan's lead to 3.
videos.headline: Ellison runs it up the gut for Hoosiers' TD

videos.description: Indiana QB Peyton Ramsey tries to find his receiver along the sideline, but Michigan's Lavert Hill cuts off the route and jumps up the pick off the pass.
videos.headline: Michigan's Hill makes leaping INT

videos.description: Trailing by three in the fourth quarter, Indiana attempts an onside kick and initially appears to recover.  However,  upon review, the recovering player's foot is just out of bounds, giving Michigan possession.
videos.headline: Indiana nearly recovers crucial onside kick

videos.description: Trailing by three with two seconds on the clock, Hoosiers' kicker Griffin Oakes sneaks a 46-yard field goal inside the right post, tying the game at 20 and forcing overtime.
videos.headline: Indiana nails field goal as time expires to force overtime

videos.description: Indiana coach Tom Allen praised Peyton Ramsey, saying the young QB is playing through an injury, but still making plays for the Hoosiers through the air and on the ground.
videos.headline: Allen: Freshman QB 'showed a lot of toughness' in first half

videos.description: Brock Huard and Bob Wischusen break down Michigan's 27-20 overtime victory over Indiana in Bloomington.
videos.headline: Defense, run game keys to Michigan win over Indiana
